preamble: The shift from the glottal fricative [h] to the open-mid front vowel [ε] is a historic vocalization, most famously seen in the evolution of the Greek letter Eta from the consonantal Heta.

examples.note: Repurposing of the glottal consonant symbol for a long open-mid vowel after the loss of the [h] sound.
Guttural interaction in segholate nouns resulting in epenthetic [ε].
